items.front: SVFR authorization: where and what is needed per 7-5-1
items.back: Surface areas of Class B, C, D, and E airspace. Pilot MUST request. At towered airports, ground visibility at least 1 statute mile for fixed-wing (helicopters exempt).

SVFR approved ONLY if IFR arrivals/departures are NOT delayed. If IFR traffic prevents SVFR: 'EXPECT (time) DELAY.' IFR departing every 2 min or arriving on final at same interval justifies delay advisory.

Apply NONRADAR or VISUAL separation between: (1) SVFR fixed-wing aircraft, (2) SVFR fixed-wing and SVFR helicopters, (3) SVFR fixed-wing and IFR aircraft. Radar separation NOT authorized for SVFR fixed-wing operations.

Do NOT assign fixed altitude. Clear at or below altitude at least 500 ft below conflicting IFR traffic, but NOT below the MSA. Reason: pilot must remain clear of clouds and needs altitude flexibility.
May authorize for specified period if aircraft can be recalled when traffic/weather requires. Where warranted and LOAs established, FSS may transmit SVFR clearances.
Authorize upon request ONLY if restricted visibility is the sole weather limitation. Aircraft maintains SVFR conditions until reaching VFR. Must be able to climb through fog/haze/smoke.

items.front: Ground visibility below 1 mile: SVFR handling per 7-5-7
items.back: Helicopters: no restriction (14 CFR 91 permits SVFR below 1 mile). Fixed-wing: advise visibility and ask intentions. Scheduled air carriers: may NOT be authorized SVFR unless pilot indicates emergency.

items.front: Flight visibility below 1 mile (no reported weather) per 7-5-8
items.back: Departing: advise if vis reported less than 1 mile, ask intentions. Arriving inside B/C/D/E: tell pilot to remain clear of clouds. Arriving outside B/C/D/E: tell pilot to remain clear of clouds and of airport traffic area.
